workiqQueriesRun: What email threads did I send or receive most frequently in the past 7 days? List subject patterns, sender/recipient groups, and approximate time spent.
Are there recurring email types I write regularly like status updates, approvals, scheduling requests, or data requests? Show examples from the past week.
Which emails required the most back-and-forth (most replies in a thread) this past week, and what was the topic?
What recurring meetings did I attend this past week? For each, what was the typical agenda type and who attends?
How much total time did I spend in meetings this past week, broken down by meeting type (1:1, team sync, external, etc.)?
Which meeting types happen every week at roughly the same time with the same people?
What Teams channels or chats am I most active in over the past 7 days? What topics come up repeatedly?
Are there questions I get asked repeatedly in Teams chats -- things people regularly come to me for?
What types of information do I most frequently share or look up in Teams conversations this week?
What types of documents did I create, edit, or review most often this past week? Any recurring doc types like reports, specs, or decks?
Are there documents I update on a regular cadence (weekly/monthly reports, trackers, dashboards)?
What SharePoint sites or OneDrive folders do I access most frequently?
Across email, meetings, and Teams, what topics or projects consumed the most of my time this week?
Are there workflows that seem to repeat where I do the same sequence of actions (e.g., get email -> schedule meeting -> create doc)?
What tasks do multiple people on my team seem to do independently that could be standardized?

signals.title: Azure DevOps Eval Work-Item Notification Triage
signals.rawExcerpt: By far the largest cluster in your last 7 days (dozens of distinct but thematically identical threads). Subject patterns: Task ####### - Eval, Requirement ####### - Evals, Bug ####### - Evaluation. You are consistently a subscriber or mentioned recipient rather than the direct assignee in many cases.

signals.title: CAPE and Evals Leadership Strategy Thread Participation
signals.rawExcerpt: Fewer threads than ADO notifications, but each includes long content, summaries, and action items. Threads: Re: CAPE East Project Crucible Deepdive Evals-first Mindset March 18; Re: Customer donated evals added Playbook; Fw: Announcing EvalCon.

signals.title: Security and Compliance Alert Review
signals.rawExcerpt: Several distinct alerts across the week. Subject patterns: Action Required: File Blocked; elevated/revoked as Administrator; Loop workspace needs your attention. Senders: Office365/Purview, Open Source Notifications, Microsoft Digital Compliance.

signals.title: Loop Workspace Collaboration Invite Dispatch
signals.rawExcerpt: At least two outbound Loop workspace invitations in the last 7 days. Subject: You have been looped in! Sent to two colleagues for the Skilluminator Workspace. Auto-generated by M365 when collaboration is initiated.

signals.title: Viva Engage and Community Digest Consumption
signals.rawExcerpt: Regular but not dominant. Subject patterns: Microsoft Daily Digest / Weekly Digest, community announcements (Copilot Tasks, Copilot Champs, Immigration Q&A, SharePoint AI tips). Passive recipient.

signals.title: High-Urgency Escalation Email Authoring
signals.rawExcerpt: One manually written high-urgency email this week. URGENT Degree CANNOT be Located sent to an external university address. PM switches into a very direct escalation style when something is blocking progress.

signals.title: Multi-Reply Strategic Thread: Project Crucible Evals-First
signals.rawExcerpt: Most discussion-heavy email conversation last week. Re: CAPE East Project Crucible Deepdive: Evals-first Mindset, Progress and Learnings March 18. Involves a very large cross-org audience (CAPE, Copilot, leadership, partners).

signals.title: Urgent Technical Coordination: Declarative Agent Architecture
signals.rawExcerpt: RE: URGENT: DBS: Declarative Agent grounded in 170+ SharePoint sites. Multiple senior stakeholders and technical owners. Strong signal of iterative clarification and coordination around architecture and readiness.

signals.title: Weekly Status Update Reporting (Parallel Team Effort)
signals.rawExcerpt: Weekly updates requested in a specific format (done/next/blockers). Separate efforts to prepare weekly sync updates, bi-weekly newsletters, Cape Day/LT slides. Teams asked multiple times to restate progress in different artifacts.

signals.title: Weekly Evals Sync (Recurring, Tuesdays 3:05-3:30 PM)
signals.rawExcerpt: Tuesdays, 3:05-3:30 PM. Weekly cadence. Same small group, same time, consistent focus on evals progress and alignment. Extended through August.

signals.title: Weekly Project Crucible v-Team Sync (Mondays 8:05-8:30 AM)
signals.rawExcerpt: Mondays, 8:05-8:30 AM. Strongly recurring with a consistent cross-team group; same slot every Monday. Extended through August.

signals.title: Weekly Manager-Led Team Meeting (Mondays 11:05-11:45 AM)
signals.rawExcerpt: Mondays, 11:05-11:45 AM. Weekly cadence. Same manager-led group, same time slot each week.

signals.title: Weekly Solution Library Check-In (Mondays 9:05-9:30 AM)
signals.rawExcerpt: Mondays, 9:05-9:30 AM. Highly regular cadence and participant set, focused on solution intake and progress.

signals.title: Learning and Scaling Fridays (Fridays 2:05-5:00 PM)
signals.rawExcerpt: Fridays, 2:05-5:00 PM. Same small group, same long learning block each Friday. Consistent group with stable participants.

signals.title: Golden Evals v-Team Bi-Weekly Review (Thursdays)
signals.rawExcerpt: Thursdays 2:05 PM, bi-weekly. Consistent eval leadership group. Secondary but shows strong repetition with same people.

signals.title: CAPE Camp AIR Immersive Training and Hackathon (Multi-Day)
signals.rawExcerpt: Two full-day events (~9 hours each, Mon and Tue) plus dozens of adjacent sessions: Workflow, Mindset, Environment Prep, M365 Copilot productivity, CRM and Dataverse integration, Hackathon standups and EOD debriefs. Accounts for ~70-80% of scheduled meeting time this week.

signals.title: External Partner and Customer Eval Sessions (50+ Domains)
signals.rawExcerpt: Total external meeting time: ~13+ hours, spread across 50+ distinct external domains. Examples: accenture.com (2h), hcltech.com (1h 50m), ey.com (55m), kpmg groups (3h total), avanade.com (1h), cognizant.com (1h).

signals.title: 1:1 Check-Ins with Stakeholders (6 per week)
signals.rawExcerpt: 6 one-on-one meetings scheduled this week, 3h 45m total. 1:1s are a relatively small slice of total meeting load (under 4 hours).

signals.title: Meeting to Notes to Follow-Up Workflow Execution (Recurring)
signals.rawExcerpt: PM repeatedly creates Loop meeting notes after meetings. Workflow: Meeting scheduled then Notes doc created then Decisions and action items captured then Follow-up work continues asynchronously. Appears across many months -- a stable pattern.

signals.title: Inbound Eval Strategy and Guidance Q&A (Repeated Pings)
signals.rawExcerpt: PM is explicitly referenced as the person doing a lot of eval guidance for FDEs and customers. Multiple chats ask PM to advise on eval depth, timing, or structure for customer sessions. Common questions: how many eval cases are enough?, what metrics to use?, when in agent lifecycle should evals happen?

signals.title: Eval Tooling CLI and Implementation Troubleshooting
signals.rawExcerpt: People come to PM for hands-on clarity when things break. Common questions: CSV import/export quirks, feature flags and preview availability, whether metrics are supported yet, how CLI eval tooling compares to in-product tooling. PM directly answering CSV formatting and tool-call accuracy flags.

signals.title: Cross-Org Navigation and VOC Routing Assistance
signals.rawExcerpt: PM explains VOC routing and DRIs for eval-related asks. People ask PM to recommend the right contacts within MCS Analytics or Eval teams. PM functioning as an informal routing layer between field, PM, and PG.

signals.title: EvalCon Content Planning and Positioning Discussions
signals.rawExcerpt: Ongoing discussions about EvalCon: whether/what CAPE should present, framing blockers and challenges, process learnings, deadlines, submissions, and alignment with leadership. Multiple threads touching strategy, framing, and deadlines across different days.

signals.title: Technical Artifact Distribution via Teams (Repos, ZIPs, Specs)
signals.rawExcerpt: A large portion of messages involve sharing concrete assets. Commonly shared: GitHub repos (Copilot SDK, skills agents, templates, playbooks), ZIP files with specs, markdown files, YAML, Loop components. PM uses Teams as a distribution hub for working artifacts.

signals.title: Agent Architecture and MCP/Skills Scoping Discussions
signals.rawExcerpt: Recurring topics: MCP servers, CLI vs UI tradeoffs, WorkIQ integration constraints, skills (skill-creator, skills-detective, PRD generator) and how agents discover or run them. One of the richest technical threads in the dataset.

signals.title: Skilluminator Cycle Dashboard Weekly HTML Refresh
signals.rawExcerpt: dashboard.html shows explicit cycle markers like Cycle 3 and Week of 2026-03-16, indicating a weekly or per-cycle refresh cadence. PM runs a repeat loop: signal ingestion to pattern detection to dashboard refresh to refinement notes.

signals.title: Agent Eval Sets Template Maintenance (Recurring Excel)
signals.rawExcerpt: Agent Eval Sets_Template.xlsx has been shared repeatedly over time (Nov 2025 to Jan 2026), functioning as a living template/tracker that gets reused and updated as eval needs evolve.

signals.title: Loop Meeting Notes Creation and Post-Meeting Action Capture
signals.rawExcerpt: PM repeatedly creates or contributes to Loop Meeting Notes files with same structure: Agenda, Meeting notes, Decisions/Open questions, Follow-up tasks. MCS Analytics and Evaluations CAPECAT-PM Review 2026-03-11.loop is a clear PM-style review and decision doc.

signals.title: Skill-Detector Technical Spec Iterative Build Cycle
signals.rawExcerpt: ZIP packages (skill-detector.zip, skill-detector 1.zip) containing SKILL.md specs plus scripts and config files (.sh, .json, .db) produced repeatedly over short intervals. Suggests recurring work on product/engineering-adjacent specs, prototypes, and local tooling.

signals.title: Parallel Eval Enablement Deck Creation (Team-Wide Duplication)
signals.rawExcerpt: Multiple versions of eval intro decks, playbooks, pitch decks, and execution plans created and shared by different people (TFM Agent Evals, Evaluation.pptx, CAPE_EVALs_Playbook_v5, eval convo decks). Meetings explicitly call out we have multiple versions floating and ask to consolidate.

signals.title: Daily Personal Day-Prep Planning Page
signals.rawExcerpt: Files like [Mar 19] Help me prepare for my day indicate a repeat personal cadence where PM updates a structured planning artifact rather than starting from scratch each time.
